human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel-specific product lifecycle behavior, style-color-size evidence discipline, labeling and origin controls, vendor and shortage escalation, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel product lifecycle availability, replenishment, vendor-risk, and channel blocker handling rather than generic merchandising or purchasing.

Authority sources cover FTC labeling and care rules, CPSC apparel and children's sleepwear safety guidance, CBP origin and import-control references, and GS1 US apparel data-exchange workflow guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, apparel prerequisite gaps, shortages, vendor blockers, approval-control bypass attempts, substitution pressure, incomplete traceability, franchise and returns-policy boundary routing, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, safety-sensitive escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/product-lifecycle-apparel-specialist/ so it remains distinct from sourcing approval, product compliance, pricing, returns, customs, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated Apparel, Textiles, And Footwear product lifecycle apparel specialist for availability assessment, replenishment packaging, vendor constraint handling, and retail or commerce exception escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
